info.name: CodeArena API
info.description:
# CodeArena API Collection

Production-ready Postman collection for the **CodeArena** LeetCode-style backend.

## Setup
1. Import this collection into Postman.
2. Set collection variables (defaults provided).
3. Run **Register** or **Login** — `accessToken` and `refreshToken` are saved automatically.
4. Use protected endpoints — Bearer auth uses `{{accessToken}}`.

## Base URL
Default: `http://localhost:3000/api`

## Problem identifiers
Use **MongoDB ObjectId** or **slug** for `problemId` (e.g. `sum-of-array-elements`).
Default slugs from dataset: EASY=`sum-of-array-elements`, MEDIUM=`find-pivot-index`, HARD=`target-sum-assign-signs`

## Code execution (C++ only)
- **Language:** C++ only — Judge0 `language_id: 54` (GCC 9.4.0)
- **Run** (`POST /submissions/run`) — judges **visible test cases only** (sync)
- **Submit** (`POST /submissions`) — judges **all test cases** via Redis worker (async)
- Execution service: CodeBox / Judge0 at `EXECUTION_API_URL` with `X-Auth-Token`
- Submit a **complete C++ program** with `main()` that reads stdin and prints stdout

## Recommended test flow
1. **Login** → saves tokens
2. **Get All Problems** → saves `problemId` + `problemSlug`
3. **Get Problem By Slug** → view hints + starter code
4. **Run Code** → test against public cases
5. **Submit Code** → full judge (start `npm run dev:worker`)
6. **Get Submission** → poll until not PENDING/RUNNING

## Auth Flow
1. `POST /auth/register` or `POST /auth/login`
2. Tokens saved to collection variables via test scripts
3. Protected routes use `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}`

## Roles
- `USER` — submit code, view problems, profile
- `ADMIN` — manage problems and tags

## Response Format
```json
{ "success": true, "data": { ... } }
```

## Error Format
```json
{ "success": false, "message": "Error description" }
```
Validation errors (400) include an `errors` array with `field` and `message`.

item.name: 1. Authentication
item.description: User registration, login, token refresh, and session management.

request.description:
Register a new user account. Returns user object, access token, and refresh token.

**Validation:**
- username: 3-30 chars, alphanumeric + underscore
- email: valid email
- password: min 8 chars

response.name: 400 Bad Request

request.description: Authenticate with email and password. Automatically saves `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables.
Invalidate the refresh token. Pass the refresh token in the request body.
Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access/refresh token pair. Old refresh token is revoked.
Get the authenticated user profile. Requires valid Bearer access token.
Get public user profile with solve statistics and recent submissions.

**Planned endpoint** — Update authenticated user profile (avatar, username).

> Not yet implemented in MVP. Included as API contract reference.

**Planned endpoint** — Change password for authenticated user.

> Not yet implemented in MVP. Included as API contract reference.

response.name: 400 Bad Request

item.name: 3. Problems
item.description: Problem discovery, search, filtering, and admin management.

request.description:
List all problems with pagination. Pass Bearer token optionally to include solved status.

**Auto-saves** `problemId` and `problemSlug` from the first result.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Get problem by **slug** (recommended). Returns description, examples, hints, C++ starter code, time/memory limits.

Test cases are only returned for ADMIN users.

response.name: 200 OK

exec: const d=pm.response.json().data; if(d?.id) pm.collectionVariables.set('problemId', d.id); if(d?.slug) pm.collectionVariables.set('problemSlug', d.slug);

request.description: Get problem by MongoDB ObjectId. Also accepts slug if passed in `problemId` variable.

response.name: 200 OK

exec: const d=pm.response.json().data; if(d?.id) pm.collectionVariables.set('problemId', d.id); if(d?.slug) pm.collectionVariables.set('problemSlug', d.slug);

request.description: Get sample EASY problem from dataset (`sum-of-array-elements`).

exec: const d=pm.response.json().data; if(d?.id) pm.collectionVariables.set('problemId', d.id); if(d?.slug) pm.collectionVariables.set('problemSlug', d.slug);

request.description: Get sample MEDIUM problem (`find-pivot-index`).
Get sample HARD problem (`target-sum-assign-signs`).
Full-text search across problem titles and descriptions.
Filter problems by difficulty: `EASY`, `MEDIUM`, or `HARD`.
Filter problems by tag (case-insensitive partial match). E.g. `dp` matches `Dynamic Programming`.
Filter by solve status: `solved` or `unsolved`. **Requires authentication** to determine user solve state.
Create a new problem. **Requires ADMIN role.** Saves returned `problemId` to collection variables.

response.name: 400 Bad Request
409 Conflict

request.description: Update an existing problem. **Requires ADMIN role.** All fields are optional.
Delete a problem permanently. **Requires ADMIN role.**

item.name: 4. Tags
item.description: Problem tag management. GET is implemented; CRUD endpoints are planned.

request.description: List all available problem tags. Tags are seeded on server startup.

exec: const tags = pm.response.json().data; if (tags?.[0]?.id) pm.collectionVariables.set('tagId', tags[0].id);

request.description:
**Planned endpoint** — Create a new tag. **Requires ADMIN role.**

> Not yet implemented in MVP.

**Planned endpoint** — Update tag name. **Requires ADMIN role.**

> Not yet implemented in MVP.

**Planned endpoint** — Delete a tag. **Requires ADMIN role.**

> Not yet implemented in MVP.

item.name: 5. Submissions
item.description: C++ code execution via CodeBox (Judge0). **Run** = visible test cases (sync). **Submit** = all test cases (async worker).

request.description:
Run C++ code against **visible test cases only** (sync). For Monaco editor "Run" button.

- Uses Judge0 `POST /submissions?wait=true` with `expected_output`
- Language: C++ only (`language_id: 54`)
- `problemId` accepts ObjectId or slug
- Code must be a complete program with `main()`

Submit C++ for **full judging** (visible + hidden test cases). Async via Redis worker — poll **Get Submission**.

Requires `npm run dev:worker` running.
Get submission details including test results. Users can only view their own submissions (ADMIN can view all).

response.name: 200 OK
403 Forbidden

request.description: Paginated submission history for the authenticated user. Filter by `status` and `problemId`.

Get the most recent submission for a problem. Uses history endpoint with `limit=1` filter.

Poll **Get Submission** after submit until status is not PENDING/RUNNING.
Check if the API server is running. Uses root URL (not `baseUrl`).
